zh.Of Tehidy, Cornwall, England: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾特希迪
zh.lifeSummary: William de Basset II是William de Basset I的兒子，於1300年9月29日出生在英國可瓦爾特希迪。他於1334年與Johanna de Botreaux在英國可瓦爾特希迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1351年4月1日在他的家鄉享年50歲。

zh-hans.Of, Tehidy, Cornwall, England: 英国英格兰可瓦爾特希迪
zh-hans.Of Tehidy, Cornwall, England: 英国英格兰可瓦爾特希迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de Basset II是William de Basset I的儿子，于1300年9月29日出生在英国可瓦爾特希迪。他于1334年与Johanna de Botreaux在英国可瓦爾特希迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1351年4月1日在他的家乡享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Basset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a diminutive of Old French bas(se) ‘short, of low stature’ (see Bas ).
English: variant of   Bassett  , a cognate of 1 above.
In some cases also an altered form of French Besset (see Bessette 2). Compare Bassette 1.

variants.name: Bas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mauricio, Abelardo, Florencio, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Soledad, Vicente.  Eastern Slavic Dmytro, Liya, Pyotr, Sergey.
Catalan: habitational name from a place so named in the district of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.
Basque: topographic name for someone who lived by a wood, from baso ‘wood, forest’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: William de Basset II was born on 29 September 1300, in Tehidy, Cornwall, England as the son of William de Basset I. He married Johanna de Botreaux in 1334, in Tehidy, Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 1 April 1351, in his hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Basset II est né le 29 septembre 1300, à Tehidy, Cornouailles, Angleterre, fils de William de Basset I. Il a épousé Johanna de Botreaux en 1334, à Tehidy, Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 avril 1351, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William de Basset II wurde am 29. September 1300 in Tehidy, Cornwall, England, als Sohn von William de Basset I geboren. Er hat 1334 in Tehidy, Cornwall, England Johanna de Botreaux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 1. April 1351 im Alter von 50 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: William de Basset II è nato il il 29 settembre 1300 nel Tehidy, Cornwall, England come figlio di William de Basset I. Lui sposato Johanna de Botreaux nel 1334, nel Tehidy, Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 aprile 1351, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Basset IIは1300年9月29日にイギリスコルンワルテヒディでWilliam de Basset Iの息子として生まれた。1334年にイギリスコルンワルテヒディでJohanna de Botreauxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1351年4月1日に故郷で50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Basset II(은)는 1300년 9월 29일 영국 코른월 테히디에서 William de Basset I의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1334년 영국 코른월 테히디에서 Johanna de Botreaux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1351년 4월 1일 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.29 September 1300–1 April 1351: 29 de setembro de 1300–1 de abril de 1351
pt.lifeSummary: William de Basset II nasceu em 29 de setembro de 1300, em Tehidy, Cornwall, Inglaterra filho de William de Basset I. Ele casou-se com Johanna de Botreaux em 1334, em Tehidy, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de abril de 1351, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William de Basset II родился 29 сентября 1300 г. в местности тэиди, корнвалл, Англия. Его отца звали William de Basset I. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna de Botreaux в 1334 г. в местности тэиди, корнвалл, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 апреля 1351 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет.

es.29 September 1300–1 April 1351: 29 de septiembre de 1300–1 de abril de 1351
es.lifeSummary: William de Basset II nació el 29 de septiembre de 1300, en Tehidy, Cornwall, Inglaterra como hijo de William de Basset I. Se casó con Johanna de Botreaux en 1334, en Tehidy, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de abril de 1351, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años.
